pattern: Track, from both directions, how long the run of equal consecutive differences is at every position; then for each index treated as the one you're allowed to overwrite, either extend one neighboring run by a single matching step, or -- when the two neighbors' target difference lines up -- bridge both runs together through the freshly chosen value.
hints: Build two arrays from the array of consecutive differences: how long the constant-difference run is ending at each index, and how long it is starting at each index.
Changing one element can always extend the run immediately to its left (or right) by exactly one step, since the replacement value is free to match whatever difference that run already has.

Changing an interior element can bridge its left and right neighbors together only if their combined gap is even, giving a single needed difference -- and even then, each side's full run only carries over if that side's own existing difference already equals the bridging difference; otherwise only the one adjacent element carries over.
The answer is the best of: the longest run with no change at all, extending a single run by one, and bridging both neighbors, maximized over every index.

edge_inputs.description: bridging the interior fixes the whole array into one run
only one side can be extended, the other side breaks off
minimum length array, already perfectly arithmetic
minimum length array, needs exactly one change to become arithmetic
no run of equal differences anywhere -- the answer still can't drop below length 2
reversing an already-arithmetic run means no change ever helps beyond its own length
the interior bridge is blocked by an odd combined gap, only single-step extension applies
two separate long runs with mismatched differences meeting at a single junction element
changing the very first or very last element can only ever extend one side

stress.description: random large array, exercising the linear scan and bridging logic at scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(4, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: mostly-arithmetic runs with a few injected disruptions, more likely to hit the bridging branch
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(4, min(size, 20000))
    nums = [0] * n
    nums[0] = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    step = rng.randint(1, 20)
    for i in range(1, n):
        nums[i] = nums[i-1] + step
        if rng.randint(0, 20) == 0:
            nums[i] = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    nums = [max(1, min(100000, v)) for v in nums]
    return [nums]

stress.description: small arrays for exhaustive brute-force cross-checking
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(4, 11)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 8) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
